pages.title: Overview
pages.body:
A 5×5 grid of cubes responds to hover, click, and keyboard events. Three color constants drive the visual feedback:

· COL_BASE — default blue
· COL_HOVER — yellow highlight on hover
· COL_CLICK — red flash when clicked

Hover a cube to highlight it. Click to launch it. Press R to reset, G to toggle the grid helper.

pages.title: Building the cube grid
pages.body:
The double loop creates GRID×GRID cubes (25 total). Each cube is positioned so the grid is centered at origin, with userData storing base position and a fly value used by the click animation.

All 25 cubes share the same geometry instance — only their materials differ. This is a common Three.js optimization.

pages.title: Raycaster setup
pages.body: The Raycaster and mouse Vector2 are created once and reused every frame. The mouse starts at (-9, -9) — well outside NDC range — so the raycaster doesn't hit anything until the user actually moves the mouse over the canvas.

pages.title: mousemove — NDC conversion
pages.body: The mouse handler converts screen pixels to Normalized Device Coordinates (-1 to +1). Critically, it uses the canvas bounding rect rather than window.innerWidth — this is required when the canvas doesn't fill the whole window, as in the Artlab IDE preview pane.

pages.title: click — launch a cube
pages.body: The click handler performs its own raycast at the moment of click (the stored mouse position can be stale). Setting cube.userData.fly = 1.0 triggers the animation in update() — the value counts down to 0 over about 1.6 seconds.

pages.title: keydown — R and G
pages.body:
The keyboard handler handles two keys:

· R — resets all cubes to their base position and color
· G — toggles a GridHelper; when toggling off, it must be removed from the scene explicitly

The handler reference is stored in ctx._onKey so it can be removed by teardown().

pages.title: update() — hover detection
pages.body:
Every frame, the stored mouse position is used to raycast against all cubes. When the hovered object changes, the previous cube is reset to its base color (only if it isn't currently flying).

The hovered cube gets COL_HOVER and a pulsing emissiveIntensity using Math.sin(t * 8) for a rapid shimmer effect.

pages.title: update() — fly animation
pages.body: Cubes with fly > 0 arc upward. The height uses Math.sin(f * PI) which gives a smooth ballistic arc — 0 at launch, peak at f=0.5, 0 at landing. Rotation is added while flying. When fly reaches 0 the cube snaps back to its base color and position.

pages.title: teardown() — event cleanup
pages.body:
All three event listeners are removed using the stored handler references. The grid helper is also explicitly removed if it was visible.

Failing to remove event listeners here would cause them to accumulate across every example load in the same session — a common source of subtle bugs.
